topic.what: Multi-Concept Compositional Unsafety (MCCU) where benign concept pairs combine to produce unsafe image content
topic.how: Introduces TwoHamsters benchmark with 17.5k prompts across 51 concept pairs to evaluate MCCU vulnerabilities in T2I models and defense mechanisms
topic.why_matters: Current safety filters fail to detect MCCU because they analyze concepts in isolation; FLUX generates unsafe content from 99.52% of MCCU prompts, revealing critical gap in T2I safety alignment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of MCCU prompts that produce unsafe outputs in FLUX model
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 - FLUX.1 achieves only 0.48% MDR meaning 99.52% generation success
characteristics.context: State-of-the-art DiT-based model exhibits near-complete vulnerability to MCCU attacks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Recall rate of LLaVA-Guard safety filter on MCCU dataset
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 - LLaVA-Guard, the best-performing safety filter, still misses 59% of MCCU cases
characteristics.context: Even VLM-based safety classifiers struggle with implicit compositional risks

characteristics.what_it_measures: MDR for SD-v1.4 showing baseline protection level
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 - Earlier U-Net architectures retain baseline MDR vs near-zero for modern DiT models
characteristics.context: Reveals inverse correlation between instruction-following quality and safety

characteristics.what_it_measures: SCR for FLUX showing preservation of single-concept generative utility
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 - FLUX maintains high utility while failing safety
characteristics.context: High utility with near-zero safety indicates optimization-target mismatch

characteristics.what_it_measures: MCCU-ViT detector recall on test set after domain-adaptive fine-tuning
characteristics.evidence: Table 5 - Near-perfect recall achieved by task-specific fine-tuned detector
characteristics.context: Demonstrates that architectural limitations are not the cause of filter failures; MCCU data scarcity is the primary factor

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean MDR variation across 10 hazard categories revealing taxonomic bias
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 - Systematic imbalance where socio-semantic categories show higher vulnerability than explicit visual hazards
characteristics.context: Current safety alignment over-focuses on explicit visual features at expense of implicit social risks

novelty: Formal definition of Multi-Concept Compositional Unsafety (MCCU) as distinct from atomic concept safety
TwoHamsters benchmark with 17.5k prompts across 51 concept pairs spanning 10 risk categories
Hierarchical taxonomy synthesizing real-world T2I platform safety policies
Three-metric evaluation framework: MDR (safety), SCR (utility), NCR (collateral damage)
open_problems: Universal MCCU defense faces semantic fluidity challenge; cultural/contextual nature of risk makes invariant filtering theoretically elusive
Current concept erasure methods cannot mitigate MCCU without catastrophic utility collapse due to distributional entanglement
Shift from concept erasure to compositional disentanglement needed; enforce conditional independence P(A|B) ≈ P(A) rather than removing concepts
Effective long-term MCCU mitigation requires persistent adaptive socio-technical approach vs one-off technical optimization
